name: Yoo et al. CASE Corruption Generation Extract
population_or_context: Rubric-based automated essay scoring for EFL writing.
sample_size: 40,185 synthetic DREsS_CASE corrupted essays in the already-published source extraction.
methods: Extracted CASE corruption as a non-LLM synthetic response generation strategy for rubric-based AES training augmentation.
education_context: Undergraduate EFL argumentative writing with content, organization, and language rubric dimensions.
response_origin: Synthetic DREsS_CASE essays are generated by corrupting essays; real DREsS_New and DREsS_Std samples provide comparator and training context.
rubric_label_space: Rubric-based content, organization, and language scores.
label_source: Generated CASE labels are aligned with target score ranges; real and standardized essays use human or dataset labels.
generation_model: CASE corruption method, not an LLM.
generation_method: Rule or procedure-based corruption to create lower-quality rubric-aligned essay samples.
evaluation_method: Automated essay scoring evaluation with real validation/test data and rubric-score agreement metrics.
real_response_comparator: Real DREsS_New essays provide a real-response split and comparator boundary.
limitations: CASE corruption differs from LLM prompt-based generation.
The synthetic subset supports training augmentation, not direct validation.
The method is bounded to EFL essay scoring and rubric dimensions reported in the source.
